ogTitle: East Munglinup vs East Nabawa: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in East Munglinup and East Nabawa.
heading: East Munglinup vs East Nabawa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between East Munglinup, Western Australia and East Nabawa, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Nabawa has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to East Munglinup (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is East Munglinup or East Nabawa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: East Munglinup has a flood risk score of 28/100, while East Nabawa scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: East Munglinup scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. East Nabawa scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in East Munglinup or East Nabawa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Nabawa has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
